prevalence.source: Orphanet; European HAE registries and epidemiology reviews
prevalence.trend: increasing diagnosed prevalence due to awareness and access to complement testing

burden.dalyPerCapita: very low (rare) but high acute morbidity; laryngeal attacks are life-threatening
burden.mortalityRate: Very low with access to on-demand therapy and emergency care

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Recurrent angioedema without urticaria; low C4; low C1 inhibitor level or function; confirm with repeat testing and genetics

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no population screening; cascade family testing is standard
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with access to on-demand therapy and prophylaxis where needed

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Complement testing and specialist allergy/immunology pathways widely available
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: On-demand therapies (C1-INH, bradykinin antagonist), prophylaxis for frequent/severe attacks, action plans and trigger avoidance
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Cross-country variation in access and reimbursement of specific therapies
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong registry networks and broad access to diagnostics and modern HAE treatments in many systems
